description: Agent extends an enum-based base (checkbox-group) by transforming each option's UI. Options are enum entries — not field models — so class-based option iteration is correct; reaction to selection flows through the parent's subscribe callback on value changes.
userMessage: Create a custom `chip-selector` component that extends checkbox-group and renders each option as a toggleable chip. Use fd:viewType `chip-selector`.

rubric.description: The scaffolder is invoked with --base checkbox-group (not --base text-input or another unrelated base).

Each option is rendered as a chip — the component iterates the option wrappers (.checkbox-wrapper) inside the register callback and swaps in a chip-styled element for each. Keeping the original checkbox UI untouched does NOT satisfy this.

The component reacts to changes in the group's value so chip state stays in sync with the underlying model — either by handling value/enum change events in the subscribe callback, or by wiring each option's input through a change event that updates the chip's selected state.
The component's subscribe call includes `{ listenChanges: true }` as required by the skill's critical rules.
